factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating as evidenced by revenue growth and increased sales volume.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 6.4% for the three months ended November 30, 2016 over the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $1,219.1 million to $1,296.9 million. Revenue increased organically by 5.7% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2015

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 5.7% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2015

factors.summary: The company is actively pursuing acquisitions, such as the acquisition of G&K Services, which is expected to add significant value.

evidence.text: The Company entered into the Agreement and Plan of Merger... for a total enterprise value of approximately $2.2 billion... See Note 9 entitled Acquisitions for additional information.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2015

factors.summary: The economic environment is favorable, supporting growth in demand.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 6.4% for the three months ended November 30, 2016 over the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $1,219.1 million to $1,296.9 million.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2015

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging through pre-payment of senior notes and managing its debt structure.

evidence.text: On June 1, 2016, Cintas paid the $250.0 million five-year senior notes that matured on that date with cash on hand and proceeds from the issuance of commercial paper. As of November 30, 2016, we had $1,044.8 million aggregate principal amount in fixed rate senior notes outstanding, net of $5.2 million of deferred financing fees, with maturities ranging from 2017 to 2036.

factors.summary: The company is actively repurchasing shares under buyback programs.

evidence.text: During the first three months of fiscal 2016, under the January 13, 2015 program, we purchased 2.4 million shares of Cintas common stock at an average price of $85.65 per share for a total purchase price of $202.5 million. From the inception of the January 13, 2015 share buyback program through the completion of the program in September 2015, Cintas purchased a total of 5.9 million shares of Cintas common stock at an average price of $84.07 per share for a total purchase price of $500.0 million.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, primarily due to expansion efforts in certain reportable operating segments.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $155.2 million and $121.8 million for the six months ended November 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively. Current year capital expenditures primarily relate to expansion efforts in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment, representing $132.6 million of the current year outflow.

factors.summary: The company faces no material litigation or regulatory risks that could significantly impact its financial performance.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to legal proceedings, insurance receipts, legal settlements and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business... the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect on the consolidated financial position or results of operation of Cintas.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is showing improvement, with a slight acceleration in the three-month period compared to the six-month period.

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 5.7% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2015

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 4.2% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2015

factors.summary: Gross margins have shown consistent expansion over the periods examined, driven by factors such as improved sourcing and warehouse efficiency.

evidence.text: The gross margin was 80 basis points higher than the prior fiscal year’s second quarter gross margin of 43.9%
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2015

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 45.9% for the six months ended November 30, 2016, which is an increase of 310 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 42.8% in the same period of the prior fiscal year. The increase was driven primarily by improved sourcing and from leveraging of existing warehouses as a result of the ZEE acquisition.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2016 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2015
